1 - Lagging: Has not established delivery cadence or prioritization frameworks; allows ad-hoc context-switching between development and support, causing paralysis.
2 - Developing: Establishes basic delivery cadence with periodic planning; inconsistently enforces prioritization, leaving team reactive to urgent issues.
3 - Proficient: Drives consistent delivery cycles (2-3 weeks) through structured planning and clear priority delegation; balances proactive feature work with reactive support effectively.
4 - Exemplary: Champions rapid, predictable delivery (1-2 week cycles) through strategic capacity planning and proactive risk management; enables both innovation and reliability simultaneously.

1 - Lagging: Fails to provide visibility into delivery timelines; roadmap changes constantly, undermining stakeholder confidence and business planning.
2 - Developing: Communicates delivery forecasts inconsistently; roadmap revisions are frequent but communicated when major delays emerge.
3 - Proficient: Provides reliable delivery forecasts; communicates roadmap adjustments promptly with clear rationale to stakeholders.
4 - Exemplary: Proactively forecasts delivery with high accuracy; transparently communicates trade-offs and adjustments; enables business planning with confidence.

1 - Lagging: Has not established code standards or quality gates; hardware integration regressions occur regularly in production deployments.
2 - Developing: Enforces basic quality practices; some hardware integration issues reach production but are addressed post-deployment.
3 - Proficient: Establishes clear code standards and testing requirements; hardware integration quality is stable with minimal field regressions.
4 - Exemplary: Institutes rigorous code reviews and testing standards; hardware integration quality is maintained at 99.5%+ reliability with zero preventable field failures.

1 - Lagging: Has not evaluated workload capacity; team is persistently understaffed or misallocated, causing burnout or idle capacity.
2 - Developing: Conducts periodic capacity reviews; workload allocation is generally appropriate but lacks strategic reserve or growth planning.
3 - Proficient: Strategically allocates team capacity based on workload analysis; maintains buffer for unexpected hardware failures and technical growth.
4 - Exemplary: Proactively right-sizes allocation based on demand forecasting; strategically balances development (2 devs) and support (7) roles to maximize efficiency and knowledge distribution.

1 - Lagging: Fails to track capacity or demand; team overwhelmed or underutilized; no contingency planning for key person dependencies (critical with 2 devs).
2 - Developing: Monitors team utilization informally; recognizes bottlenecks reactively but lacks systematic capacity planning.
3 - Proficient: Maintains visibility into team capacity through structured tracking; proactively manages workload to prevent burnout and key person risks.
4 - Exemplary: Strategically manages capacity across development and support; proactively addresses knowledge concentration risks with 2 devs through cross-training and documentation.

1 - Lagging: Has not provided necessary development or support tools; team operates with ad-hoc processes and manual workarounds.

3 - Proficient: Ensures effective tool utilization (ClickUp for ticket tracking, Google Workspace for collaboration); identifies and resolves process bottlenecks.
4 - Exemplary: Proactively invests in tooling that removes friction and enables team scaling; drives continuous improvement in hardware development and support workflows.

1 - Lagging: Communicates infrequently or unclearly; stakeholders lack visibility into Hardware Head's priorities, challenges, or status.

4 - Exemplary: Proactively shares transparent communication with stakeholders; articulates strategy, trade-offs, and team wins regularly; builds trust through clarity.
